concerns.text: T006 (synthetic_query.py) explicitly states it 'MUST call code/generators/reference_engine.py (T007)' to assign ground_truth_plan. However, T007 is marked [P] (parallel) and listed alongside T006 in Phase 2. This creates a semantic ordering violation: a consumer (T006) is scheduled to run before or concurrently with its producer (T007). T007 must be a strict prerequisite for T006.

T008 (base executor) states it 'MUST utilize the throttling mechanism implemented in T016'. T016 is marked [P] and listed in the same Phase 2 block. If T008 runs in parallel with T016, the import/usage of the throttling logic will fail. T016 must precede T008.

T024 (plan comparison) depends on 'ground_truth_plan generated by T006/T007'. While T006/T007 are in Phase 2, T024 is in Phase 4 (US2). The dependency is correctly ordered across phases, but the description in T006 (see first concern) breaks the chain. If T006 fails to generate the plan due to the T007 race condition, T024 cannot function.

T030 (visualization) depends on 'aggregated data from T021 and T026'. T021 (US1) and T026 (US2) are in different user story phases. While the phase ordering (US1/US2 before US3) is correct, the task description implies a hard dependency on the *completion* of US2 (T026) before US3 (T030) can start. If the team intends to run US1 and US3 in parallel (as suggested by the 'Parallel Team Strategy' section), T030 cannot start until T026 finishes. This contradicts the 'independent' nature of US3 if it requires US2's data aggregation.
